page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m2.medium is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance within the ecs.m2 server family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores with 4 vCPUs, operating with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, which indicates that hyperthreading is disabled. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per physical core.

This instance configuration does not include local storage, requiring external block storage volumes for operating system and data storage needs. Additionally, the ecs.m2.medium does not feature any integrated graphics processing units or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The lack of local storage and hardware acceleration limits its use to workloads that rely on network-attached storage and CPU-only execution.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.m2.medium offers a high memory-to-vCPU density. This resource profile is designed for memory-intensive applications that do not require massive parallel thread execution or high-performance local disk I/O. Appropriate workloads include medium-sized databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise applications, and memory-bound backend services.
description: The Alibaba Cloud ecs.m2.medium is an x86_64 architecture instance within the ecs.m2 family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs, featuring a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio that indicates hyperthreading is not active. The instance provides 32.0 GB of system memory, establishing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. This resource configuration is designed for memory-optimized workloads, offering qualitative cost efficiency for applications requiring high memory density relative to compute resources. Appropriate workloads include in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications that demand significant memory capacity without requiring high-density parallel processing or local disk performance.
